overrides.override_summary: Post-term non-compete may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA Business and Professions Code generally prohibits non-compete agreements. Franchisees in CA may not be bound by the 2-year, 15-mile post-term restriction.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot waive claims under CA Franchise Investment Law (Corp Code §31512) or Franchise Relations Act (B&P Code §20000-20043)
overrides.why_it_matters: Release signed at renewal or transfer cannot extinguish CA franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: FL governing law provision may not be enforceable to the extent it conflicts with CA Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA law may supersede FL choice of law for CA franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire or acknowledgment may waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee's ability to bring fraud claims.

overrides.override_summary: Bankruptcy termination provision may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee filing bankruptcy may be protected from automatic termination.

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the Franchise Agreement
overrides.why_it_matters: FL choice of law is overridden; IL franchise law protections apply.

overrides.override_summary: Any provision designating jurisdiction or venue outside Illinois is void (Section 4, IL Franchise Disclosure Act)
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in FL. Arbitration outside IL is still permitted.

overrides.override_summary: Any provision purporting to waive compliance with IL Franchise Disclosure Act is void (Section 41)
overrides.why_it_matters: Full protection under IL franchise law cannot be contractually waived.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring lawsuit in Maryland for claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees have venue option in home state for MD law claims.

overrides.override_summary: General release required at renewal/transfer does not apply to liability under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchise law claims survive the general release.

overrides.override_summary: Claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides any shorter contractual limitation period.

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination required (with 60 days to cure). 180 days notice for non-renewal.
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer notice periods than FA provides. Gives MN franchisees more time to cure defaults or prepare for non-renewal.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require franchisee to assent to general release, assignment, novation, or waiver relieving any person from liability under MN §§80C.01-80C.22 (Rule 2860.4400D)
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise law claims cannot be released at renewal or transfer.

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages provision may not be enforceable under Minnesota law
overrides.why_it_matters: The complex liquidated damages formula (up to 36 months of fees) may be void in MN.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota or require waiver of jury trial or consent to liquidated damages (Minn. Stat. §80C.21, Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: FL venue, jury waiver, and liquidated damages provisions are all potentially unenforceable for MN franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Payment of initial franchise fee deferred until franchisee is open for business
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees do not pay the $60,000 IFF until studio actually opens, reducing pre-opening capital requirements.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must indemnify MN franchisees against third-party trademark infringement claims arising from authorized use of Marks
overrides.why_it_matters: Reverses the FA's general disclaimer of trademark protection obligation.

overrides.override_summary: General release at renewal/transfer does not waive claims under NY GBL Article 33 (Sections 687(4) and 687(5))
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law claims survive the general release.

overrides.override_summary: NY Franchise Law governs any claim arising under that law
overrides.why_it_matters: FL choice of law cannot override NY franchise law for claims arising under NY GBL.

overrides.override_summary: Fee deferral until franchisor completes pre-opening obligations under franchise agreement
overrides.why_it_matters: VA franchisees do not pay IFF (and MUD dev fee) until franchisor fulfills its pre-opening duties.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot cancel franchise without reasonable cause under VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564
overrides.why_it_matters: Some non-curable default triggers in the FA may not constitute 'reasonable cause' under VA law.

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act (RCW 19.100) prevails in event of conflict
overrides.why_it_matters: FL choice of law is overridden where it conflicts with WA franchise law.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee Bill of Rights (RCW 19.100.180) supersedes FA termination/renewal provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law provides additional protections on termination and renewal that override FA terms.

overrides.override_summary: Treble damages available under RCW 19.100.190; punitive/exemplary damages waiver is void
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees can seek up to 3x damages. The FA's punitive damages waiver is unenforceable in WA.

overrides.override_summary: Any requirement to purchase or rent for more than fair and reasonable price is unlawful (RCW 19.100.180(2)(d))
overrides.why_it_matters: Mandatory vendor pricing (LeadTeam, call center, software) must be fair and reasonable in WA.

overrides.override_summary: Provisions permitting franchisor to repurchase franchisee's business without consent during term are unlawful unless termination for good cause (RCW 19.100.180(2)(j))
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor's book-value purchase option on termination/expiration may be limited in WA.

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire/acknowledgment may waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance. Cannot prohibit communication with regulators.
overrides.why_it_matters: Comprehensive anti-waiver protections for WA franchisees.
